sections.paragraph: (“Bel’s prince,” i.e. prince whom Bel favours, compare בֵּלֹ; tsha, a termination which is added to words in the Zendic as a mark of the genitive, and zar=sar, prince), Belteshazzar, the Assyrio-Babylonic name of Daniel in Nebuchadnezzar’s court, Dan 1:7 2:26 4:5 , , 15 , 16 10:1 .
